citation_and_locator: Cheung, Lawrence Yam-Leung. 2009. Dislocation focus construction in Chinese. Journal of East Asian Linguistics 18:197-232. DOI 10.1007/s10831-009-9046-z; open-access eScholarship postprint, especially pp.197-216 and examples 3-6, 33 onward
what_it_supports: Direct Cantonese canonical/medial-particle contrasts; syntactic dependency supported by four diagnostics; context-sensitive focus interpretation; multiple constituent sizes; heaviness and speaker-variation qualifications.
limit: The movement, head-initial CP, and Spine Constraint account is theory-specific; one author's judgments do not establish unrestricted productivity or modern population-wide acceptance.

citation_and_locator: Yap, Foong Ha, Tak-sum Wong, and Winnie Oi-wan Chor. 2014. Clause-medial particles and stance marking in Cantonese. PolyU institutional slide deck, slides 4, 28-33, 37
what_it_supports: Establishes a separate clause-medial stance/parenthetical research boundary involving lexical-to-stance developments.
limit: A presentation on stance marking does not establish DFC, focus movement, or a general medial-particle rule.
